mission.title: crossplane: Deleting a provider should not delete all managed resources
mission.description: Deleting a provider should not delete all managed resources. Requested by 17+ users.

steps.title: Check current crossplane deployment
steps.description:
Verify your crossplane version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
helm list -n crossplane 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working crossplane installation.

steps.title: Review crossplane configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant crossplane configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get configmap -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=crossplane
```
### What problem are you facing?

When a provider is deleted it deletes all the managed resources.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Deleting a provider should not delete all managed resources
steps.description:
Hey  ! The ticket you shared captures a lot of what's going on in the situaion when things get stuck which is also something i was able to observe. However it states that:
```
Any MRs will be left in an orphaned, unreconciled, un-deletable state.
```

This reads like MR's are not deleted when in fact i observed situations where they actually get deleted which is why i created this ticket. If we can add that behavior to the ticket you shared i think we can close this one. Thank you for the quick

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get events -n crossplane --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Deleting a provider should not delete all managed resources" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hey  ! The ticket you shared captures a lot of what's going on in the situaion when things get stuck which is also something i was able to observe. However it states that:
```
Any MRs will be left in an orphaned, unreconciled, un-deletable state.
```

This reads like MR's are not deleted when in fact i observed situations where they actually get deleted which is why i created this ticket.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with crossplane installed or the issue environment reproducible.
